title: Building images in container fails (regression 1.28)
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**
Running buildah in a fedora:36 container in a GitLab pipeline fails since Fedora 36 updated buildah from 1.27 to 1.28.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
See [commands in script](https://gitlab.com/kwinft/ci-images/-/blob/f66e0e59af29f4f2925be1c697eee9cabc2769b1/build-img.sh#L54) run in the Fedora image. The script being copied and then with bash executed is [this](https://gitlab.com/kwinft/ci-images/-/blob/f66e0e59af29f4f2925be1c697eee9cabc2769b1/archlinux/frameworks-master.sh), but probably that doesn't matter.



**Describe the results you received:**
```
buildah run base-working-container bash /opt/buildah.sh
error running container: did not get container start message from parent: EOF
Error: failed to read from slirp4netns sync pipe: EOF
```
[Full Pipeline log](https://gitlab.com/kwinft/ci-images/-/jobs/3322603456)

**Describe the results you expected:**
Downgrading temporarily the package manually (via rpmfind.net) fixes the problem:[ pipeline log](https://gitlab.com/kwinft-ci-bench/ci-images/-/jobs/3324605128#L473)

comments.body: Hi @subdiff , Does every command fail ? Could try a simpler command like `./buildah run <ctr-name> echo hello` , also could you confirm if both packages upgrades or downgrades the version of `slirp4netns` as well ?
Please also share output of `slirp4netns -v` after and before upgrading buildah from `1.27` and `1.28`

I'm not completely sure if it's the same issue, but `buildah manifest create` broke for me when upgrading from 1.27.2 to 1.28.0 As far as I can tell, the bug was introduced by e3d48600. Building and running `buildah manifest create` fails at that commit, but it works one commit previously:

```
> docker run --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --security-opt apparmor=unconfined -v /Users/matthew:/matthew --rm -it ghcr.io/flox/flox
bash-5.1# cd /matthew/buildah
bash-5.1# git checkout e3d48600
HEAD is now at e3d48600 vendor: update c/common
bash-5.1# (cd /matthew/nixpkgs && nix-build -A buildah)
...build output...
bash-5.1# /matthew/nixpkgs/result/bin/buildah manifest create flox-manifest
Error: mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: operation not permitted                            
ERRO[0000] [graphdriver] prior storage driver overlay failed: mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: operation not permitted                                          
WARN[0000] failed to shutdown storage: "mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: operation not permitted"
bash-5.1# git checkout HEAD~                             
Updating files: 100% (397/397), done.                       
Previous HEAD position was e3d48600 vendor: update c/common        
HEAD is now at 94ab98d6 cmd: check for user UID instead of privileges          
bash-5.1# (cd /matthew/nixpkgs && nix-build -A buildah)                   
...build output...                                               
bash-5.1# /matthew/nixpkgs/result/bin/buildah manifest create flox-manifest
3599b2111ede29301aa5632804cd138939c78636a3ea5066a2583b9ae072df16
```
Most likely a change in containers storage since the mount command is failing.

I'm not getting the error if I revert https://github.com/containers/storage/commit/975fab1023f74c7b9787323deca2250a82469e98 (ping @giuseppe )

I built buildah using https://github.com/mkenigs/buildah/commit/6a60d87e20f32c6d544c7ccbca4b25ae8d0a6447

> I'm not completely sure if it's the same issue, but `buildah manifest create` broke for me when upgrading from 1.27.2 to 1.28.0 As far as I can tell, the bug was introduced by [e3d4860](https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/e3d4860067a4442ef7b83908f473180f760c27cf). Building and running `buildah manifest create` fails at that commit, but it works one commit previously:
> 
> ```
> > docker run --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --security-opt apparmor=unconfined -v /Users/matthew:/matthew --rm -it ghcr.io/flox/flox
> bash-5.1# cd /matthew/buildah
> bash-5.1# git checkout e3d48600
> HEAD is now at e3d48600 vendor: update c/common
> bash-5.1# (cd /matthew/nixpkgs && nix-build -A buildah)
> ...build output...
> bash-5.1# /matthew/nixpkgs/result/bin/buildah manifest create flox-manifest
> Error: mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: operation not permitted                            
> ERRO[0000] [graphdriver] prior storage driver overlay failed: mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: operation not permitted                                          
> WARN[0000] failed to shutdown storage: "mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: operation not permitted"
> bash-5.1# git checkout HEAD~                             
> Updating files: 100% (397/397), done.                       
> Previous HEAD position was e3d48600 vendor: update c/common        
> HEAD is now at 94ab98d6 cmd: check for user UID instead of privileges          
> bash-5.1# (cd /matthew/nixpkgs && nix-build -A buildah)                   
> ...build output...                                               
> bash-5.1# /matthew/nixpkgs/result/bin/buildah manifest create flox-manifest
> 3599b2111ede29301aa5632804cd138939c78636a3ea5066a2583b9ae072df16
> ```

FYI
Yes, my pipeline, which only does `buildah manifest create` also started failing since 1.28 upgrade.
I fixed it just setting `STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs` explicitly. My container is not configured to leverage overlay.

I am affected by this (or at least something that looks very similar), too. I’m running Buildah in a GitLab CI Kubernetes runner, inside of an unprivileged container, without FUSE.

Using `--isolation chroot --storage-driver vfs`, I am able to build container images on 1.27, but on 1.28 I get errors like these:

```console
$ buildah build --isolation chroot --storage-driver vfs --tag example-image:v1.28 .
STEP 1/3: FROM python
Resolved "python" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/python:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:0f67f32c26d393a2580062f2cebfde80cc4c5a5e264bbb7a32569c6c7551c1c2
[… some more blobs …]
Copying config sha256:75cc8d87cc34bdb26dfcbe6c9e4483d9b483761510b5069852f52bd8431b8012
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2/3: RUN pip install rich
error running subprocess: creating new mount namespace for [/bin/sh -c pip install rich]: operation not permitted
Error: building at STEP "RUN pip install rich": exit status 1
```

I’m using the Quay.io images for Buildah. You can see the output of an example run, with 1.27 succeeding and 1.28 failing, in the GitLab CI job output I’m attaching below (collapsed). It also includes `slirp4netns -v`, as requested by @flouthoc above.

Both a `buildah build --isolation chroot --storage-driver vfs .` as well as a `buildah run --isolation chroot --storage-driver vfs $(buildah from --storage-driver vfs --pull python) python --version` work on 1.27 and fail on 1.28.

Let me know if I can provide you with any more input.

<details>
<summary>.gitlab-ci.yml</summary>

```yaml
build_image_1_27:
  stage: build
  image: quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.27
  script:
    - slirp4netns -v
    - buildah build --isolation chroot --storage-driver vfs --tag example-image:v1.27 .

build_image_1_28:
  stage: build
  image: quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.28
  script:
    - slirp4netns -v
    - buildah build --isolation chroot --storage-driver vfs --tag example-image:v1.28 .

run_cmd_1_27:
  stage: build
  image: quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.27
  script:
    - slirp4netns -v
    - buildah run --isolation chroot --storage-driver vfs $(buildah from --storage-driver vfs --pull python) python --version

run_cmd_1_28:
  stage: build
  image: quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.28
  script:
    - slirp4netns -v
    - buildah run --isolation chroot --storage-driver vfs $(buildah from --storage-driver vfs --pull python) python --version
```

</details>

So glad I found this. Read up on all the blog posts, best practises and whatnot; to have it not work. While it used to work fine. Blew away my entire Podman using `podman system reset` as well and had a look at the `mount_program` in `/etc/containers/storage.conf`; which is commented and setting it to `mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"` did not change the error at all.

Ran into this issue while setting up new Gitlab Runner (using the Docker runner (`docker-ce-20.10.22-3.el7.x86_64`)) to build container images with. Which failed promptly on me. Configurating a gitlab runner to use rootless Podman did not work either. To diagnose the issue I decided to effectively run the same container on my laptop (Fedora 36, `podman-4.3.1-1.fc36.x86_64`). Ran into the same issue. The `quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.28` breaks for me:

```
$ podman run  --rm -it --device /dev/fuse:rw --security-opt seccomp=unconfined quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.28 buildah info
Error: mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: operation not permitted
WARN[0000] failed to shutdown storage: "mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: operation not permitted" 
```

I tried to add `--security-opt label:disable` but that had no effect. I left out my volume mount of making a local directory available inside the container at `/var/lib/containers/`.

The same command with `quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.27` gives me:
```
$ podman run  --rm -it --device /dev/fuse:rw --security-opt seccomp=unconfined  quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.27 buildah info
{
    "host": {
        "CgroupVersion": "v2",
        "Distribution": {
            "distribution": "fedora",
            "version": "36"
        },
        "MemFree": 3300306944,
        "MemTotal": 16525242368,
        "OCIRuntime": "crun",
        "SwapFree": 8589930496,
        "SwapTotal": 8589930496,
        "arch": "amd64",
        "cpus": 4,
        "hostname": "3cc629a7367e",
        "kernel": "6.0.18-200.fc36.x86_64",
        "os": "linux",
        "rootless": true,
        "uptime": "2h 3m 18.18s (Approximately 0.08 days)",
        "variant": ""
    },
    "store": {
        "ContainerStore": {
            "number": 0
        },
        "GraphDriverName": "overlay",
        "GraphOptions": [
            "overlay.imagestore=/var/lib/shared",
            "overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs",
            "overlay.mountopt=nodev,fsync=0"
        ],
        "GraphRoot": "/var/lib/containers/storage",
        "GraphStatus": {
            "Backing Filesystem": "btrfs",
            "Native Overlay Diff": "false",
            "Supports d_type": "true",
            "Using metacopy": "false"
        },
        "ImageStore": {
            "number": 0
        },
        "RunRoot": "/run/containers/storage"
    }
}
```
On my laptop the container image `quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.27` seems to work. At least `buildah info` seems happy. I will try `v1.27` on the runner to see how far I get with that.

If I can provide other information or test things I would be happy to.
